Title: Cell-type-aware regulatory landscapes governing monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis in the medicinal plant Catharanthus roseus

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates desacetoxyvindoline to form deacetylvindoline in the late vindoline pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an immediate precursor toward vindoline/vinblastine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its expression is activated by IDM1 in idioblast-associated tissue, supporting late-stage monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer bisindole alkaloid used in human chemotherapy
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Idioblast cells in leaf and petal for late-pathway activity; petals also accumulate vindoline, anhydrovinblastine, and vinblastine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Idioblast-specific in leaves; also strongly upregulated in petal idioblast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate inducibility discussed for the pathway/regulator context; transient Agrobacterium infiltration in petals used for validation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: single-nuclei RNA-seq + single-nuclei ATAC-seq, NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA1098712; petal overexpression RNA-seq (same study, BioProject PRJNA1098712); Figshare 10.6084/m9.figshare.25586979
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single-cell multiome profiling; IDM1 overexpression followed by RNA-seq in petals; reporter transactivation assay with accessible promoter region
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D4H is a canonical idioblast-specific late MIA biosynthetic gene. 2. IDM1 overexpression increased D4H transcript abundance in petals. 3. IDM1 directly transactivated an accessible upstream D4H promoter region, supporting D4H as a direct IDM1 target.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native accessible promoter region tested with minimal 35S reporter; 35S used for IDM1 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Idioblast-specific accessible chromatin region upstream of D4H is associated with its expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful late-pathway target for increasing vindoline/vinblastine branch output, especially in combination with idioblast regulator IDM1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper treats D4H mainly as a downstream biosynthetic target rather than the central experimental gene.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal vindoline-forming step of late branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Terminal known step toward vindoline in idioblast
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms vindoline, a direct precursor contributing to vinblastine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its expression is activated by IDM1 in idioblast-associated tissue, supporting terminal vindoline formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vindoline is a monomeric precursor required for formation of the anticancer bisindole alkaloid vinblastine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Idioblast cells in leaf and petal for late-pathway activity; petals also accumulate vindoline, anhydrovinblastine, and vinblastine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Idioblast-specific in leaves; strongly upregulated in petal idioblast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate inducibility discussed for the pathway/regulator context; transient Agrobacterium infiltration in petals used for validation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: single-nuclei RNA-seq + single-nuclei ATAC-seq, NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA1098712; petal overexpression RNA-seq (same study, BioProject PRJNA1098712); Figshare 10.6084/m9.figshare.25586979
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single-cell multiome profiling; IDM1 overexpression followed by RNA-seq in petals; reporter transactivation assay with accessible promoter region
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DAT is an idioblast-specific terminal vindoline-pathway gene. 2. IDM1 overexpression increased DAT expression in petals. 3. IDM1 directly transactivated an accessible DAT upstream region, supporting DAT as a direct IDM1 target.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native accessible promoter region tested with minimal 35S reporter; 35S used for IDM1 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Idioblast-specific accessible chromatin region at the DAT promoter is associated with its expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for boosting vindoline/vinblastine branch output when paired with idioblast-specific activation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper treats DAT mainly as a direct target gene of IDM1.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates expression of late-stage MIA biosynthetic genes D4H and DAT and an idioblast-specific metabolic regulon
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vindoline contributes to vinblastine biosynthesis; vinblastine is an anticancer alkaloid used in chemotherapy
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Idioblast cells; petals also accumulate vindoline, anhydrovinblastine, and vinblastine
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Exclusively expressed in idioblast cells of leaves; most specifically detected in petal idioblast among candidates; induced by methyl jasmonate (log2FC 5.4)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Idioblast-specific in leaves and petal idioblast
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate induces IDM1; plants grown under 14-h photoperiod at 22°C; functional tests performed by Agrobacterium-mediated transient petal expression
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Activates other idioblast TF candidates (IDW1 and IDM2/3), suggesting feedforward activation within an idioblast regulon
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates D4H and DAT; also upregulates IDW1, IDM2, and IDM3
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No protein complex experimentally demonstrated in this study
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: First documented idioblast-specific transcription factor regulating two idioblast-specific late MIA biosynthetic genes; decisive idioblast regulator for late vindoline branch expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single-cell multiome analysis; motif enrichment and co-expression analysis; transient overexpression in petals followed by RNA-seq; nuclear localization; reporter transactivation assays with DAT and D4H accessible promoter regions
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: single-nuclei RNA-seq + single-nuclei ATAC-seq, NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA1098712; petal overexpression RNA-seq, PRJNA1098712; Figshare 10.6084/m9.figshare.25586979
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: D4H promoter accessible region; DAT promoter accessible region
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising idioblast-specific engineering lever to activate late vindoline/vinblastine biosynthesis; likely useful in stacking with upstream regulators such as MYC2/ORCA/BIS to cover early and late pathway sectors
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear growth or yield penalty reported; activates broader idioblast metabolic regulon so off-target specialized metabolic changes are possible
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; DAT and D4H native accessible promoter fragments fused to minimal 35S reporter in transactivation assays
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Candidate targets are associated with idioblast-specific accessible chromatin regions; IDM1 acts through accessible cis-regulatory elements upstream of D4H and DAT
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Late-stage monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis and idioblast-specific metabolic program
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IDM1 was identified by integrating idioblast-specific chromatin accessibility, motif enrichment, and co-expression. 2. IDM1 is an idioblast-specific, JA-inducible R2-R3 MYB TF. 3. Overexpression of IDM1 specifically activates D4H and DAT, unlike IDW1 or IDM2/3. 4. IDM1 directly transactivates accessible promoter regions of D4H and DAT. 5. IDM1 also activates an idioblast-enriched metabolic regulon, establishing it as a novel late-pathway regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper names this factor Idioblast MYB1 (IDM1); in Discussion it is also referred to as CrIDM1.
